docs.title: About Me
docs.text:
I am a Chief Data Engineer at Deutsche Post DHL Group and an Assistant Professor of Computer Science at Universum College.

I obtained my PhD from University of Bonn in the Smart Data Analytics (SDA) group under the supervision of Prof. Dr. Jens Lehmann. Before that, I completed my MSc at the University of Prishtina, supervised by Prof. Dr. Lule Ahmedi.

My research and engineering interests include Semantic Web, Big Data, Machine Learning, and distributed systems such as Apache Spark and Apache Flink.

docs.title: Interests
docs.text:
• Big Data

  • Semantic Web and Semantic Search

docs.title: Summary
docs.text: Chief Data Engineer at Deutsche Post DHL Group and Assistant Professor at Universum College, focused on building scalable data platforms and leading big data and semantic technology initiatives from strategy through delivery.

docs.title: At A Glance
docs.text:
• Current roles: Chief Data Engineer, Assistant Professor
  • Primary focus: Big data engineering, distributed systems, semantic technologies
  • Core stack: Spark, Flink, Kubernetes, Airflow, OpenSearch, ClickHouse, Kafka/Redpanda
  • Location: Bonn, Germany

docs.title: Data Programming - Universum College
docs.text:
This course teaches core programming concepts in Python with emphasis on real data manipulation tasks from science, engineering, and business. By the end of the course, students can independently implement useful programs from a data source and problem description.

Terms: WiSe 2021/22

docs.title: Lab "Distributed Big Data Analytics" (MA-INF 4223) - University of Bonn
docs.text:
The lab provides hands-on experience with big data processing tools such as Apache Spark and introduces functional programming patterns used in concurrent and parallel big data systems.

Terms: SoSe 2019, WiSe 2018/19, SoSe 2018, WiSe 2017/18, SoSe 2017

docs.title: Set Up SeaTunnel with Kubernetes
docs.text: This post provides a quick guide to running SeaTunnel with Kubernetes.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Install and verify the following tools on your local system:

  • Docker
  • Kubernetes
  • Helm

For local Kubernetes, this setup uses Minikube v1.23.3:

minikube start --kubernetes-version=v1.23.3

docs.title: SeaTunnel Docker image
docs.text:
Create Dockerfile:

FROM flink:1.13

ENV SEATUNNEL_VERSION="2.1.0"

RUN wget https://archive.apache.org/dist/incubator/seatunnel/${SEATUNNEL_VERSION}/apache-seatunnel-incubating-${SEATUNNEL_VERSION}-bin.tar.gz
RUN tar -xzvf apache-seatunnel-incubating-${SEATUNNEL_VERSION}-bin.tar.gz

RUN mkdir -p $FLINK_HOME/usrlib
RUN cp apache-seatunnel-incubating-${SEATUNNEL_VERSION}/lib/seatunnel-core-flink.jar $FLINK_HOME/usrlib/seatunnel-core-flink.jar

RUN rm -fr apache-seatunnel-incubating-${SEATUNNEL_VERSION}*

Build and load the image:

docker build -t seatunnel:2.1.0-flink-1.13 -f Dockerfile .
minikube image load seatunnel:2.1.0-flink-1.13

docs.title: Deploy the Flink Kubernetes Operator
docs.text:
Install cert-manager (once per cluster):

kubectl create -f https://github.com/jetstack/cert-manager/releases/download/v1.7.1/cert-manager.yaml

Install the operator:

helm repo add flink-operator-repo https://downloads.apache.org/flink/flink-kubernetes-operator-0.1.0/
helm install flink-kubernetes-operator flink-operator-repo/flink-kubernetes-operator

Check status:

kubectl get pods

docs.title: Run SeaTunnel Application
docs.text:
Use a simple streaming config in flink.streaming.conf:

env {
  execution.parallelism = 1
}

source {
  FakeSourceStream {
    result_table_name = "fake"
    field_name = "name,age"
  }
}

transform {
  sql {
    sql = "select name,age from fake"
  }
}

sink {
  ConsoleSink {}
}

Prepare host path on Minikube:

minikube ssh
sudo mkdir /mnt/data

Copy config file:

minikube cp flink.streaming.conf /mnt/data/flink.streaming.conf

Create seatunnel-flink.yaml:

apiVersion: flink.apache.org/v1alpha1
kind: FlinkDeployment
metadata:
  namespace: default
  name: seatunnel-flink-streaming-example
spec:
  image: seatunnel:2.1.0-flink-1.13
  flinkVersion: v1_14
  flinkConfiguration:
    taskmanager.numberOfTaskSlots: "2"
  serviceAccount: flink
  jobManager:
    replicas: 1
    resource:
      memory: "2048m"
      cpu: 1
  taskManager:
    resource:
      memory: "2048m"
      cpu: 2
  podTemplate:
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: flink-main-container
          volumeMounts:
            - mountPath: /data
              name: config-volume
      volumes:
        - name: config-volume
          hostPath:
            path: "/mnt/data"
            type: Directory
  job:
    jarURI: local:///opt/flink/usrlib/seatunnel-core-flink.jar
    entryClass: org.apache.seatunnel.SeatunnelFlink
    args: ["--config", "/data/flink.streaming.conf"]
    parallelism: 2
    upgradeMode: stateless

Deploy:

kubectl apply -f seatunnel-flink.yaml

docs.title: Output and Monitoring
docs.text:
Follow logs:

kubectl logs -f deploy/seatunnel-flink-streaming-example

Expose dashboard:

kubectl port-forward svc/seatunnel-flink-streaming-example-rest 8081

Open http://localhost:8081.

TaskManager logs:

kubectl logs -l 'app in (seatunnel-flink-streaming-example), component in (taskmanager)' --tail=-1 -f

Stop and clean up:

kubectl delete -f seatunnel-flink.yaml

A simplified version of this guide has been contributed to SeaTunnel.
